evidence_excerpt: Diagnosed with familial pseudohyperkalemia (temperature-dependent potassium leak) and discharged; no treatment or complications reported (“We explained these results to her and her parents, and she was then discharged.”).

quality_of_life.evidence_excerpt: No symptoms reported and no abnormalities on examination; condition recognized as an in vitro artifact (“…hyperkalemia without any symptoms… Electrocardiography revealed no signs of hyperkalemia… She was then discharged.”).

notes_on_uncertainty: No explicit post-discharge follow-up details are provided. Long-term outcome and quality-of-life assessments are inferred from the benign, asymptomatic nature of familial pseudohyperkalemia and the fact that the patient was discharged without treatment; the condition is a persistent laboratory artifact rather than a clinical disease.
